If the error banner is present, it momentarily becomes the first focal point in the main area, appearing above both columns and pushing the layout down, then the eye resumes the normal left-to-right, top-to-bottom flow.

layout.overall_description: The Booking Details page opens with a full-width, fixed header bar spanning the entire top of the viewport, using a muted, sophisticated background color and centered navigation that anchors the user in the booking process. Below this header, a generous band of whitespace (about 56px) separates it from the main content, which sits in a white, card-like band centered on the page within a max-width container. If present, a slim, understated error banner spans the full width of this container at the very top of the content band. Beneath it, the layout resolves into a two-column grid floating on the white background: on the left, a narrow column holds a bold yet refined 'Booking Details' heading, a short, calm explanatory sentence, and a compact slot summary panel that reads like an editorial info card. On the right, a wider column holds the booking form. This form starts at nearly the same vertical height as the page title, reinforcing the visual connection, and runs downward in a clean stack of full-width fields with measured vertical spacing. The primary action button sits at the bottom left of the form column, aligned with the form field edges. The overall effect is an airy, editorial-style layout where the right-hand form is clearly the main action, while the left-hand text and summary provide reassuring context without crowding the page.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: This routing-state handler is a non-visual infrastructure component. It does not render any UI or occupy physical space in the layout. Conceptually, it sits at the top of the page’s logic hierarchy, controlling which booking details are shown, but it remains invisible and does not affect spacing or alignment of visible elements.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Non-visual; no spatial footprint; operates entirely in the background without affecting the document flow.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The data-loader normally remains invisible and takes up no space. When the page is in a loading state (for example, fetching the selected booking slot details), it triggers a semi-transparent overlay that covers the entire main content area (the white band below the header, including both left and right columns) but stops at the edges of the centered max-width container. A subtle, centered spinner or loading indicator is placed in the middle of this container, roughly centered both horizontally and vertically in the visible portion of the main content. The overlay sits above the two-column layout, dimming the form and summary panel while preserving their underlying structure for context.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Non-visual in normal state; in loading state, behaves as an absolute-positioned overlay relative to the main content container, covering it with a translucent layer and centered indicator while not shifting underlying layout.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The booking slot summary panel is placed in the left column of the main content area. The main content container is centered on the page, spanning roughly 80–90% of the viewport width up to a max-width of about 1040–1200px. Within this container, the left column occupies about 35–40% of the width. At the top of this left column, the page title and a short description appear; directly beneath that descriptive text, with about 24px of vertical gap, the summary panel appears as a compact card-like block. It spans nearly the full width of the left column, leaving a small inner padding margin (around 16–24px) from its edges, and has a subtle border or soft background tint to differentiate it from the white page background. Vertically, it occupies enough height to comfortably list key slot details (date, time, location, guest count) without feeling crowded, but it remains shorter than the full height of the form on the right, so visually the form column extends further downward. The summary panel aligns with the left edge of the page title above and sits with at least 48px of padding from the bottom of the content band, preserving the spacious, editorial feel.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static within the left column; it participates in the normal document flow, shifting downward if the error banner appears above the columns or if the heading/description content grows.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: This form-state manager does not render visual elements itself and occupies no visible space. It sits logically behind the booking details form in the right column, tracking field values and interaction states without altering the layout. All visual feedback is expressed through the form and the error banner rather than this component directly.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Non-visual; purely logical; does not influence spacing or positioning of UI elements.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The booking details form sits in the right column of the main content area, which occupies roughly 60–65% of the centered max-width container. Horizontally, the form aligns flush with the right column’s left edge, spanning nearly the full width of that column so that its input fields read as full-width elements. The top of the form is aligned close to the vertical baseline of the page title on the left (within about 8–16px), so when viewed side by side, the heading and the first form field (or form section label) appear to start at almost the same height, reinforcing a coherent top row across both columns. Inside the form, fields are stacked vertically with consistent spacing: approximately 16px vertical gap between individual fields (e.g., Name, Email, Phone) and 24–32px gaps between logical groups (e.g., contact info group, event details group, special instructions). Each input has comfortable internal padding (around 8–12px vertically and 12–16px horizontally), with labels positioned either above the fields or as subtle placeholders, ensuring short line lengths and readability. The entire form block maintains at least 48px of padding from the bottom of the main content band. On taller forms, the right column extends below the end of the left column, but the vertical rhythm remains even, forming a clean downward reading path to the primary action button at the bottom.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static within the right column; scales vertically with content, pushing the primary action button downward as fields are added. On smaller viewports, it can reflow to appear above or below the summary panel in a single-column stack while keeping the same vertical order: heading, summary, form.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The validation manager is an invisible logic component responsible for the state of errors in the form. It does not draw any visible fields or indicators itself and does not alter spacing directly. Instead, it determines when validation messages should appear within or around the form fields and when the global error banner should be shown, allowing those visual elements to integrate into the existing vertical rhythm without adding separate structures.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Non-visual; operates behind the scenes; any spatial effects are realized through error states rendered inside the form or via the error banner.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The error banner appears only when triggered (for example, after a failed form submission). It is placed at the top of the main content area, inside the centered max-width container, spanning its full width from left to right. Vertically, it sits just below the header bar, with about 48–56px of whitespace separating it from the header to preserve the airy feel. There is then a 32px gap between the bottom of the banner and the start of the two-column layout (title and form). The banner is a low-profile horizontal strip with a muted but noticeable background color (e.g., desaturated red or warm tone), moderate padding (around 12–16px on all sides), and text that wraps on short line lengths to maintain readability. It does not overlap the underlying content; instead, it pushes the entire two-column block downward when visible, maintaining clean stacking order and consistent left/right alignment with the content columns.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static within the main content flow when present; inserted above the two-column layout. It does not float or stick; it scrolls away with the content as the user scrolls.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The submission controller is a non-visual component coordinating what happens when the user presses the primary action button. It does not render any UI and occupies no visible layout space. Its influence is expressed through the form’s loading state, any resulting error banner, and navigation changes, rather than through its own visual presence.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Non-visual; strictly background logic; no direct spatial behavior.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The navigation handler orchestrates the behavior of the top navigation but does not itself draw the header. Conceptually, it is tied to a full-width, fixed-position header bar that spans 100% of the viewport width and sits pinned to the very top. Inside that bar, navigation items are centered horizontally within a max-width container that aligns with the main content below. The header height occupies roughly 15–20% of the initial viewport height, with navigation links evenly spaced horizontally (16–24px between items) and vertically centered within the bar. The active or current step (Booking Details) is indicated with a subtle underline or a minimal style change that matches the refined aesthetic. This entire header remains fixed as the user scrolls, while the main content scrolls beneath it.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Behaves as a fixed-position header at the top of the viewport, with navigation items centered; remains visible while the main content area scrolls underneath.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The primary action button (e.g., 'Continue to Summary' or 'Review Booking') is placed at the bottom of the booking details form in the right column. It aligns with the left edge of the form fields and uses a width that matches the visual weight of the design: on larger screens, it may be a fixed width (e.g., 200–260px) that does not span the entire column, while still leaving generous whitespace to its right; on smaller devices, it can stretch to full column width for ease of tapping. There is a 24–32px vertical gap between the last form field and the top of the button. Below the button, another 32–48px of whitespace separates it from the bottom edge of the main content band, ensuring it does not feel cramped against the page edge. The button’s color stands out within the otherwise muted palette but remains sophisticated, and its height follows the vertical rhythm, with around 12–16px of internal vertical padding so it appears substantial without being bulky.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static element at the end of the form’s content flow. It moves downward if additional fields are added above. It does not stick to the viewport; it scrolls naturally with the form.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The page-state manager is an invisible controller that coordinates global states such as loading, error presence, and whether the summary or confirmation views should be shown. It sits conceptually above the main content band, but it does not render any visible UI or consume layout space. All of its effects are realized through the visibility and behavior of other components, such as the loader, error banner, and navigation.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Non-visual; purely logical; does not directly participate in layout or document flow.

name: Booking Summary
title: Booking Summary
description: Displays the finalized summary of a single booking (date, time, guest count, event details, and contact information) using the provided bookingId, as the last non-payment review step in the booking flow.

core_features: Load booking data by bookingId from the URL using getBookingSummary and render all relevant event and contact details.
Display a structured, read-only summary of the booking: event date, start/end time, guest count, event name, event type, and primary contact details.
Clearly indicate that the selected time slot for the specified number of guests has been captured, based on the Booking record returned.

Provide actions for the user to either go back to edit booking details (previous step), return to the availability calendar (book another date), or optionally complete/acknowledge this non-payment step.
Handle error cases where bookingId is missing, getBookingSummary fails, or the booking cannot be found, with options to retry or navigate back to availability.
Support bookmarking and direct access via booking_summary.html?bookingId=... with consistent behavior (load and display the corresponding Booking).
user_workflows:
Workflow 1: Review immediately after filling booking details
1. User completes booking details on booking_details.html and submits the form.
2. The booking flow redirects to booking_summary.html with a bookingId URL parameter.
3. On page load, the Booking Summary page reads bookingId from the URL.
4. The page calls getBookingSummary(bookingId).
5. While data is loading, a loading state is shown.
6. Once the Booking is loaded, the page displays: event date, start time, end time, guest count, event name, event type, and contact details.
7. User reviews the information:
   - If the information is correct, the user may optionally click a 'Finish' or 'Confirm' action (implementation-dependent) or simply leave the page knowing the booking is captured.
   - If some information is incorrect, the user clicks an 'Edit Details' action to return to booking_details.html, passing along the same bookingId.
8. If the user wants to plan another event, they click 'Book Another Date' / 'Back to Calendar' to navigate to availability.html.

Workflow 2: User revisits the summary from a bookmark or email link
1. User opens a link to booking_summary.html with bookingId in the query string.
2. The page reads bookingId from the URL.
3. The page calls getBookingSummary(bookingId).
4. The booking information is shown as in Workflow 1.
5. The user may verify their booking details or use navigation actions:
   - Edit details (back to booking_details.html with bookingId).
   - Book another date (availability.html).
   - Optionally use any confirmation/acknowledgement action if implemented.

Workflow 3: Error handling when booking cannot be loaded
1. User navigates to booking_summary.html without a bookingId parameter, or with an invalid bookingId.
2. The page attempts to read bookingId from the URL and detects it is missing or invalid.
3. The page either:
   - Does not call getBookingSummary if bookingId is missing, or
   - Calls getBookingSummary and receives an error / null-equivalent result.
4. The page displays an error state describing that the booking summary cannot be loaded.
5. User options in error state:
   - Click 'Retry' if a bookingId exists but loading failed, which re-triggers getBookingSummary with the same bookingId.
   - Click 'Back to Calendar' to go to availability.html if they want to start over.
   - Optionally navigate back in browser history.
interactions: On page load: Read the bookingId query parameter from the URL and, if present, trigger getBookingSummary(bookingId).
On successful data load: Bind returned Booking fields (eventDate, startTime, endTime, guestCount, eventName, eventType, contactName, contactPhone, contactEmail, status) to summary display components.
On loading error (network or API error): Show an error message and a 'Retry' button. Clicking 'Retry' invokes getBookingSummary again with the same bookingId.
On missing bookingId: Skip API calling, show a dedicated message indicating that no booking was specified, and display actions to navigate back to availability.html.
On 'Edit Details' click: Navigate to booking_details.html with ?bookingId=<current bookingId> so the booking details form can be edited for this same booking.
On 'Book Another Date' / 'Back to Calendar' click: Navigate to availability.html (no additional required parameters).

On optional 'Finish' / 'Confirm' click (if implemented in integration): Trigger a client-side event that can be wired to subsequent flow (e.g., navigation to a confirmation or dashboard page). This component itself does not call any additional interfaces beyond getBookingSummary.

On optional 'Print / Save' click: Call window.print() or equivalent client-side print behavior to allow the user to print or save the summary as a PDF. This does not involve additional data interfaces.

On status-based messaging: If Booking.status exists, the page can conditionally display a status indicator (e.g., draft/pending_review/confirmed/cancelled) using the value from the Booking object, with no hardcoded assumptions about what each status means beyond its label.
page_functionality.state_logic:
The primary page state is driven by the bookingId URL parameter and the result of getBookingSummary:
- URL Parameter: bookingId
  - Required for normal operation of this page.
  - If bookingId is present, the page attempts to load booking data via getBookingSummary(bookingId).
  - If bookingId is missing, the page enters a 'no booking' error state and suggests returning to availability.html.

- Loading States:
  - Initial: When the page first loads and bookingId is available, a 'loading' state is set while getBookingSummary is in progress.
  - Success: When getBookingSummary returns a valid Booking, the state transitions to 'loaded', and all summary components receive the Booking data.
  - Error: If getBookingSummary fails (e.g., network error or invalid ID) or returns no Booking, the state transitions to 'error'. In 'error' state, the page shows an appropriate message with options to Retry or go Back to Calendar.

- Data-derived State:
  - Date/Time State: eventDate, startTime, endTime from Booking define the displayed event date/time slot; the page does not compute or assume day-of-week or period but may display these values in a user-friendly format using client-side formatting logic.
  - Guest Count State: guestCount from Booking is used to indicate the confirmed guest capacity for this booking.
  - Contact & Event Details: eventName, eventType, contactName, contactPhone, and contactEmail are read-only values displayed to the user.
  - Status State: Booking.status (draft, pending_review, confirmed, cancelled) may be used to display a status indicator or note. The page does not modify status using any additional interface.

- Navigation and State Reset:
  - Changing the bookingId in the URL (e.g., via navigation or direct URL change) should reset the page state to 'loading' and trigger getBookingSummary for the new bookingId.
  - Navigation to other pages (availability.html, booking_details.html) is done via full page reload with appropriate URL parameters; no state is persisted via localStorage or other client storage mechanisms.

No additional application state is persisted outside of the current page lifecycle. All dynamic content comes from getBookingSummary and the current URL parameters.

layout.grid_system: Implicit 12-column grid with a constrained max-width content area centered in the viewport. The left text block occupies roughly 4 columns and the right form block around 8 columns.
layout.layout_pattern: Maintain the same linear, top-to-bottom structure as the mockup: a full-width fixed header at the very top, then a generously spaced main content band centered in the viewport. Inside the main area, use a two-column layout that mirrors the original text + form pattern. The left column (roughly 4 of 12 columns) holds the 'Booking Summary' title, a short explanatory paragraph, and any high-level booking status or reassurance copy. The right column (roughly 8 of 12 columns) replaces the form stack with a vertically structured summary stack: grouped booking details (date/time, guest count, event type, menu selection, contact info, special requests) presented as segmented blocks, aligned to form a strong top-to-bottom reading path.
